Name: nm-mlops
Description:
The application will be utilized by the Red Hat AI team to support model optimization and algorithmic research, including quantization and pruning. The network connectivity between GCP and IBM Cloud is essential to allow us to expand to the A100 GPU cloud resources in IBM.
A100 GPUs are essential to efficiently quantize large-scale LLMs and Mixture of Experts (MoEs) models with hundreds of billions of parameters, ensuring feasible runtimes for these computationally intensive tasks.
Architecture type: Internal (INTRANET facing), target audience RH Associates (FTEs/Contractors)

Name: 3scale SaaS
Description:
Application programming interfaces (APIs) are key to agile integration and delivering business value in a digital world. Support innovation, enable cross-enterprise agility, and create new products and revenue streams.

Red Hat® 3scale API Management makes it easy to manage your APIs. Share, secure, distribute, control, and monetize your APIs on an infrastructure platform built for performance, customer control, and future growth. Place 3scale components on-premise, in the cloud, or on any combination of the two.

ARR-002 - Errata Tool Automation (we consolidated under this CMDB entry)
ARR-004 - OSCI Pipelines
ARR-006 - Gating Bot
ARR-007 - OSCI Jenkins
ARR-011 - artifacts.osci.redhat.com

DIST-013 - distrolibrium

Over time we will gradually decommission these systems as they become obsolete before eventually retiring ARR-002 completely.

The goal of this feature is to provide tailored forward-looking roadmap information to RHEL customers, as well as providing tailored information on how current RHEL minor and major releases will affect the customers environment.  

This will enable customers to confidently understand the impact of upgrading systems (for both minor and major releases) in their environment (including positive and negative impacts), and will enable them to better understand what is coming in future RHEL releases so that they can better plan and provide feedback to Red Hat early if needed.

Name: ship-help slack bot
Description:
SHIP is an organization in Hybrid Platforms responsible for the OpenShift product pipeline (CI, Testing, Build, and Release). Product engineers regularly need to navigate this organization to find answers relevant to the domain. The @ship-help bot is designed to provide interactive guidance to these engineers, answering common questions, and steering them to appropriate resources whenever possible.
The implementation utilizes approved generative Gemini AI models and team specific grounding data to provide this guidance. The AIA assement for this behavior is AIA-397 (approval details https://docs.google.com/document/d/153A5-549EqQRuI2QlHQEjntBhZsDAneODUHus8REs-U/edit?tab=t.0 ). The Slack application itself is hosted in a Red Hat managed public cloud account.
Architecture type: Internal (INTRANET facing), target audience RH Associates (FTEs/Contractors)

Name: Routing Lookup Subgraph
Description:
The Routing Lookup subgraph will be responsible for resolving any incoming URLs (legacy or current) to its canonical content reference, specifically the content ID and content type (Article or Solution).

Its primary purpose will be to centralize how routing and redirects are handled across the KBase. So instead of having the FE infer whether a URL is valid, legacy, or needs to redirect, the Routing Lookup subgraph will provide a single queryable source of truth that maps all possible URL forms to their final destination.

Name: UserTesting Technologies
Description: UserTesting Technologies (UTT) is a full-stack user research tool. The UXD Research team plans to use use the research tooling to conduct both moderated and unmoderated studies (including video, audio, and screen sharing with consent from participants). They will also use a component of their tooling, EnjoyHQ, for data warehousing, analysis, synthesis, tagging, and internal sharing.  In May 2024, we received an approved PIA to include integration with Slack and Jira. Slack channel will be used by the UXD team to inform Red Hatters of insights gained from research studies that would be beneficial for making key product decisions. The integration with Jira will allow the UXD team to track the progress of Jira issues they have opened as a result of findings that were identified within the research studies.

Name: XE AI KCS Drafting API
Description: Our solution utilizes AI to streamline the creation of Knowledge-Centered Service (KCS) artifacts by aiding associates in generating initial drafts from available case details. This tool integrates with existing platforms, including Salesforce Classic, CaseView Plus, Customer Portal Drupal, and Cassie (formerly KBedit), to improve the drafting process. The final tool for deployment will be chosen based on stakeholder consensus and technical feasibility, guaranteeing smooth integration into current workflows.
Architecture type: Internal (INTRANET facing), target audience RH Associates (FTEs/Contractors)
